title: The Coherence Thesis
subtitle: Five-Minute Overview of the nine published manuscripts.

nodes.title: The substrate
nodes.summary: Civilization rests on a substrate. Extraction spends down the living ground it needs. Coherence keeps physiology, relationship, intelligence, and institutions aligned enough to act under pressure.

nodes.title: Intelligence in relationship
nodes.summary: Intelligence is relational, not private property. Humane technology strengthens the conditions that let people think, trust, regulate, and coordinate together.

nodes.title: The coordination device
nodes.summary: Providence names the coordination problem without flinching. Trust, care, wisdom, and action must become legible and portable without becoming coercion or surveillance.

nodes.title: The architecture under test
nodes.summary: Sequence, funding, governance, technology, practice, and place turn Providence from thesis into a design that can be tested. The volume distinguishes proposals from construction and names what remains unbuilt.

nodes.title: The human center
nodes.summary: The series returns to the person. Contribution becomes possible when human capacities are recognized, cultivated, and connected without treating people as resource deposits.

nodes.title: The smallest nest
nodes.summary: The architecture contracts to its smallest working container. Current, gift, pathway, seeing, people, loom, currency, right size, dragon, and nest become one portable pattern.

nodes.title: The praxis
nodes.summary: Volume VII calls presence, presencing, initiation, mentorship, retreat, and holding internal technologies. It proposes ways to cultivate them while making consent, appeal, exit, and safeguarding part of the practice.

nodes.title: The reckoning
nodes.summary: The extractive order already has an architecture. This volume surveys its effects, separates evidence from interpretation, and contrasts it with a more accountable direction. Its factual accounting still requires claim-level verification.

nodes.title: A proposed first Scale
nodes.summary: The Cardinal Scale is presented as a place where the thesis might be tested. Land, Doors, Membrane, currency, governance, and community remain design commitments until the site, law, safeguards, and implementation are verified.
